residential_recording_structure.principle: Use flexibly — not as a forced heading list every time.
steps: What did the child say, show or communicate?
What did adults do to support?
What follow-up, oversight or escalation is needed?
wording_discipline: Record behaviour as communication where appropriate.
Name adult actions specifically, not just 'staff supported'.
Include outcome and next steps where known.
State when information is unknown rather than inventing it.
Keep records factual, warm and child-centred.
Separate observation from interpretation — use 'appeared', 'presented as', 'said', 'showed', 'staff observed', 'not yet known'.
Include child voice, presentation, wishes and feelings where appropriate — do not invent them.
Record the child's experience before, during and after adult support.
Name adult actions specifically — how staff listened, what was offered, de-escalation, repair and plan follow-through.
Avoid vague 'staff supported', 'staff managed' or 'staff dealt with it' unless specific adult actions follow.
If adult response is missing, prompt for it rather than fabricating actions.
Reframe judgemental rough-note wording into observable, respectful language — do not pass blaming phrases through to final records.
Separate observation from interpretation; avoid labels such as manipulative, attention-seeking or kicked off.
Follow local safeguarding procedures; advise escalation without making the safeguarding decision.
Do not conclude 'no safeguarding concern' — record what is known and what remains unclear.
Adults remain accountable for professional judgement, escalation and final records.
Help adults consider management oversight — pattern, plan review, manager/senior review, supervision/debrief/learning, handover and follow-up.
Use 'manager/senior should consider reviewing…' not 'manager must conclude…'. ORB supports oversight; it does not complete management oversight.
Avoid compliance guarantees, claims of regulatory preparedness, or 'no further action' without responsible adult review.

record_types.label: General Dictation
record_types.description: Rough shift notes converted to professional wording without a fixed form.
record_types.purpose: Capture informal notes and convert them to clear professional language.
record_types.when_to_use: When you have rough notes, voice memos or quick observations that need professional wording.
record_types.when_not_to_use: When a specific incident, safeguarding or regulatory record is required — choose a dedicated template.
child_voice_checks: What the young person said or communicated
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Use observable facts, avoid judgement, name adults and times where known. Include child voice/presentation, specific adult actions, outcome and follow-up where relevant.
suggested_follow_up_actions: Choose a specific record type if needed
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required before use as a formal record.

record_types.label: Daily Record
record_types.description: Shift events, child presentation and follow-up in professional wording.
record_types.purpose: Document routine and notable events during a shift.
record_types.when_to_use: After key interactions, routines, outings or notable presentation during a shift.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For serious incidents, safeguarding concerns or missing episodes — use a dedicated template.
child_voice_checks: Direct quotes or communication where appropriate
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Observable facts, child-centred language, times and adults named.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required.

record_types.label: Incident Reflection
record_types.description: Factual incident account with safeguarding and manager oversight.
record_types.purpose: Structure a clear incident record for review and escalation.
record_types.when_to_use: After behavioural incidents, injuries, damage, absconding attempts or serious dysregulation.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For routine daily events or low-level interactions.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Chronological facts, no blame language. Include child voice/presentation, specific adult de-escalation actions, outcome, repair and follow-up.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review and escalation per policy required.

record_types.label: Missing From Home Record
record_types.description: Missing-from-care episode and return conversation with contextual safeguarding.
record_types.purpose: Record absence, return and exploitation indicators professionally.
record_types.when_to_use: When a young person has been absent, missing, returned late or returned with concerns.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For general absence from school only — use education template unless care placement risk applies.
child_voice_checks: Child account of absence and return
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Factual timeline, non-judgemental return conversation, exploitation lens.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — follow missing-from-care policy and notify per procedure.

record_types.label: Safeguarding Reflection
record_types.description: Safeguarding concern with child voice, immediate safety and rationale.
record_types.purpose: Document a concern with clear escalation pathway.
record_types.when_to_use: When a concern arises about harm, neglect, exploitation or professional boundaries.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For routine behaviour management without safeguarding meaning.
child_voice_checks: Child words in quote marks where possible
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Facts led, no speculation. Record child words, adult response, escalation pathway, outcome and follow-up. Adults decide — ORB does not make safeguarding decisions.
final_document_headings: Action taken in line with policy
Further escalation or management oversight needed
pdf_heading_order: Action taken in line with policy
Further escalation or management oversight needed
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — follow safeguarding policy immediately if child at risk.

record_types.label: Physical Intervention
record_types.description: Physical intervention or restraint with injury check and debrief requirements.
record_types.purpose: Record restraint/intervention factually with regulatory oversight.
record_types.when_to_use: After any physical intervention, hold or restraint used to manage risk.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For verbal de-escalation only — use incident or daily record.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Objective sequence, least restrictive language, no blame.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — notify per restraint policy; adult review mandatory.

record_types.label: Key-work Summary
record_types.description: Planned direct work session with child voice and plan link.
record_types.purpose: Document therapeutic or key work contact and outcomes.
record_types.when_to_use: After a planned key work, life story, goals or direct work session.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For unplanned incidents — use incident or daily record.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Strengths-based, child-led language. Include child voice, emotional support offered, specific adult actions, outcome and contact plan follow-up.
final_document_headings: What the child shared or communicated
pdf_heading_order: What the child shared or communicated
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required.

record_types.label: Management Oversight Note
record_types.description: Manager review, professional curiosity and required actions.
record_types.purpose: Capture manager oversight, decisions and action ownership.
record_types.when_to_use: When reviewing records, practice or child experience as a manager.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For frontline daily logs — use daily record unless adding manager layer.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Fact-led oversight with child impact, staff actions observed, outcomes and follow-up actions with owners. No inspection grade predictions.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — manager remains accountable for decisions.

record_types.label: Chronology Entry
record_types.description: Dated chronology entry with significance and plan impact.
record_types.purpose: Add a significant event to the child's chronology.
record_types.when_to_use: When an event should be tracked on the chronology with clear significance.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For routine daily detail — use daily record unless chronology-worthy.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Concise, significance clear, impact on child explicit.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — chronology entries require adult verification.

record_types.label: Handover Note
record_types.description: Safe shift handover with risks, events and plans.
record_types.purpose: Support continuity between shifts.
record_types.when_to_use: At end of shift or before handover to the next team.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For detailed incident accounts — attach or link incident record.
required_sections: Risks or vulnerabilities for next shift
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Concise handover: child presentation and voice, what adults did that helped, end-of-shift outcome, risks and clear follow-up actions for incoming staff.
final_document_headings: Risks or vulnerabilities for next shift
pdf_heading_order: Risks or vulnerabilities for next shift
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — verbal handover still required per policy.

record_types.label: Education / School Refusal
record_types.description: School attendance, refusal or education engagement record.
record_types.purpose: Document education-related presentation and actions.
record_types.when_to_use: When recording school refusal, attendance issues or education meetings outcomes.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For missing from placement — use missing from home record.
child_voice_checks: Child view on school and barriers
manager_oversight_checks: Education lead / manager informed if persistent
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Non-blaming, trauma-informed, barriers not deficits.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — coordinate with designated teacher and social worker.

record_types.label: Health / Medication Note
record_types.description: Health presentation, medication or clinical contact record.
record_types.purpose: Document health events, medication and clinical follow-up.
record_types.when_to_use: After health concerns, medication administration issues or clinical appointments.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For MAR chart completion — use authorised MAR process.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Clinical accuracy, no diagnosis unless qualified, factual observations.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — not a substitute for clinical records or MAR.

record_types.label: Family Contact Record
record_types.description: Family contact session with child voice and impact.
record_types.purpose: Record supervised or supported family contact.
record_types.when_to_use: After family contact sessions, phone calls or significant family interactions.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For professional meetings without family — use appropriate template.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Balanced, child-centred. Include child voice, how adults supported, outcome after contact and follow-up for contact plan review.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — follow contact plan and safeguarding policy.

record_types.label: Allegation Against Staff
record_types.description: Initial factual record for allegations — LADO pathway preparation.
record_types.purpose: Capture facts without prejudice pending investigation.
record_types.when_to_use: When an allegation against staff or volunteer requires documentation.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For general complaints — use complaint template unless allegation threshold met.
manager_oversight_checks: Senior manager and DSL informed immediately
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Neutral, factual, no conclusions, preserve evidence.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — follow allegations policy immediately; do not investigate informally.

record_types.label: Complaint or Child Concern
record_types.description: Complaint or child-raised concern with response pathway.
record_types.purpose: Document complaints and child concerns with clear response.
record_types.when_to_use: When a child, family or stakeholder raises a concern or complaint about care.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For staff allegations — use allegation template.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Respectful, non-defensive, rights-based language.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — follow complaints and safeguarding policies.

record_types.label: Risk Assessment Update
record_types.description: Risk assessment review or update with evidence link.
record_types.purpose: Document changes to known risks and mitigations.
record_types.when_to_use: After incidents or reviews that change risk profile.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For event narrative only — create incident record first.
child_voice_checks: Child view on risk where appropriate
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Evidence-based, specific mitigations, dated review.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — formal risk assessment requires authorised sign-off.

record_types.label: Care Plan Update
record_types.description: Care plan change proposal with child participation evidence.
record_types.purpose: Capture proposed care plan updates from practice evidence.
record_types.when_to_use: When practice evidence suggests care plan goals or strategies should change.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For single events — use daily or incident record unless plan impact is clear.
manager_oversight_checks: Manager review before formal plan change
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Strengths-based, specific, linked to outcomes.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — formal care plan changes require statutory process.

record_types.label: Social Worker Update
record_types.description: Concise update for allocated social worker or reviewing officer.
record_types.purpose: Summarise significant developments for children's services.
record_types.when_to_use: When practice requires social worker awareness or decision.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For internal-only handover — use handover note.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Concise, professional, fact-led with clear actions requested.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — verify recipient and consent before sharing.

record_types.label: Reg 40 Notification Prep
record_types.description: Preparation notes for serious event notification (Reg 40).
record_types.purpose: Gather facts for regulatory notification decision.
record_types.when_to_use: When preparing a potential Reg 40 serious event notification.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For routine incidents below notification threshold.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Precise facts for regulatory audience, no speculation.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — Reg 40 decisions require registered manager and legal advice.

record_types.label: Regulation 44 Preparation
record_types.description: Independent visitor evidence summary for Reg 44.
record_types.purpose: Summarise practice evidence for independent scrutiny visit.
record_types.when_to_use: When preparing evidence for Reg 44 independent visitor.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For day-to-day recording — use daily or specific templates.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Evidence-linked summary of child experience, practice observed, adult actions, outcomes and follow-up. Balanced — no compliance guarantees.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — Reg 44 report is completed by independent visitor.

record_types.label: Regulation 45 Reflection
record_types.description: Quality of care reflection linked to Reg 45 standards.
record_types.purpose: Capture reflective learning from practice and inspection themes.
record_types.when_to_use: After Reg 45 reviews, quality audits or significant learning events.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For incident facts — use incident record first.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Reflective not defensive, learning-focused, child impact centred.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — reflection supports but does not replace formal QA records.

record_types.label: Behaviour Reflection
record_types.description: Reflect on behaviour as communication with context, adult response and learning.
record_types.purpose: Understand behaviour in context and plan supportive responses.
record_types.when_to_use: After dysregulation, repeated patterns or behaviour that needs reflective recording.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For serious incidents requiring full incident reflection — use Incident Reflection.
manager_oversight_checks: Manager review if pattern or risk increases
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Non-blaming, trauma-informed, behaviour as communication. Include child voice/presentation, specific adult response, outcome and learning follow-up.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required; not a diagnostic record.

record_types.label: Supervision Preparation
record_types.description: Prepare reflective supervision with child-centred practice focus.
record_types.purpose: Structure issues, reflections and support needs before supervision.
record_types.when_to_use: Before supervision to organise practice reflection and child-centred concerns.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For formal supervision record — use after the session.
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Honest reflection, child impact centred, support-seeking tone.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — for preparation; supervision record remains adult responsibility.

record_types.label: Action Plan
record_types.description: Child-centred improvement plan with owners, timescales and review.
record_types.purpose: Turn identified needs into clear actions for the child.
record_types.when_to_use: When agreeing improvements, follow-up or multi-agency actions for a child.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For event narrative only — capture the event first.
child_voice_checks: Child involved in plan where appropriate
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Specific, achievable, child-outcome focused.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — formal plans require authorised sign-off.

record_types.label: Meeting Notes
record_types.description: Structured notes from team meetings, shift discussions and internal practice meetings.
record_types.purpose: Capture meeting discussion with child-centred professional structure.
record_types.when_to_use: After team meetings, shift discussions, practice meetings or internal planning conversations.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For formal supervision records — use supervision discussion template after the session.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Use child-centred, factual language. Attribute views to speakers where known. Do not invent names, decisions or actions.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.

record_types.label: Professional Consultation
record_types.description: Notes from professional consultations with clinicians, education or external specialists.
record_types.purpose: Document consultation content with clear child-centred focus.
record_types.when_to_use: After clinical, educational, therapeutic or specialist professional consultations.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For informal chats without professional purpose — use general dictation.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Use child-centred, factual language. Attribute views to speakers where known. Do not invent names, decisions or actions.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.

record_types.label: Home Visit Note
record_types.description: Notes from home visits, family contact sessions or visits in the community.
record_types.purpose: Record home visit or family contact discussions with child voice and safeguarding focus.
record_types.when_to_use: After home visits, family contact sessions, community visits or placement visits.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For routine in-home daily events — use daily record.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Factual visit record with child voice, how adults supported during the visit, observable presentation, outcome and follow-up. Observation before interpretation.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.

record_types.label: Assessment Notes
record_types.description: Notes from assessments, reviews or structured professional discussions about a child.
record_types.purpose: Structure assessment discussions with evidence-led, child-centred headings.
record_types.when_to_use: After assessment meetings, review discussions or structured professional assessments.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For final signed assessments — this is a draft capture aid only.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Use child-centred, factual language. Attribute views to speakers where known. Do not invent names, decisions or actions.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.

record_types.label: Supervision Discussion
record_types.description: Record of reflective supervision or practice supervision discussions.
record_types.purpose: Capture supervision discussion with practice reflection and child-centred focus.
record_types.when_to_use: After supervision sessions, practice discussions or reflective supervision meetings.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For pre-supervision preparation only — use supervision preparation template.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Reflective, supportive supervision record. Include child-centred practice discussed, adult responses reviewed, outcomes agreed and follow-up actions.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.

record_types.label: Multi-agency Discussion
record_types.description: Notes from multi-agency meetings, LAC reviews and partner discussions.
record_types.purpose: Document multi-agency discussion with child voice and agreed actions.
record_types.when_to_use: After LAC reviews, multi-agency meetings, partner strategy discussions or network meetings.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For pre-meeting preparation — use strategy / multi-agency prep.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Child-centred, factual multi-agency summary. Include child voice, home team adult response, impact on the child, agreed outcomes and follow-up. Attribute views to agencies.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.

record_types.label: Strategy / Safeguarding Discussion
record_types.description: Notes from strategy discussions or safeguarding meetings where carefully governed recording is required.
record_types.purpose: Capture strategy or safeguarding discussion themes with clear boundaries — draft for adult review.
record_types.when_to_use: After strategy discussions, safeguarding meetings or high-level risk planning conversations you are authorised to record.
record_types.when_not_to_use: For active child protection investigations — follow local authority guidance; do not use ORB to make findings.
child_voice_checks: Child's voice, wishes or presentation included where discussed
record_types.professional_language_guidance: Factual safeguarding strategy record. Include child welfare, adult support offered, escalation pathway, human decisions and time-bound follow-up. ORB does not make safeguarding decisions.
record_types.safety_disclaimer: Draft only — adult review required. ORB supports recording and reflection; it does not replace professional judgement or safeguarding procedures.
